title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: creation reactablePlus, schoolhistory package becomes thin domain layer Imports: reactablePlus. retains school-history-specific configuration demo apps.

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
code: # BEFORE school_history_config <- function() {   table_config(     columns = list(       col_spec("school", "school_picker", "School", ...),       col_spec("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", ...),       ...     ),     ...   ) }  school_history_ui <- function(id) {   editable_table_ui(id, school_history_config()) }  school_history_server <- function(id, ...) {   editable_table_server(id, school_history_config(), ...) }  # AFTER school_history_config <- function() {   table_config(     columns = list(       widget_col("school", "school_picker", "School", ...),       widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", ...),       ...     ),     ...   ) }  school_history_ui <- function(id) {   config_table_ui(id, school_history_config()) }  school_history_server <- function(id, ...) {   config_table_server(id, school_history_config(), ...) }

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: Replace instances : - library(schoolhistory) → library(reactablePlus) (add ) - useSchoolHistory() → useReactablePlus() - col_spec(...) → widget_col(...) - editable_table_ui(id, config) → config_table_ui(id, config) - editable_table_server(...) → config_table_server(...)

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
code: Imports:     reactablePlus,     shiny,     bslib,     dplyr,     reactable

title: Migration Guide: schoolhistory Downstream Package
text: completing renames, run existing shinytest2 suite: tests test-shinytest2.R pass without modification function renames applied, since test end--end behavior rather internal function names.
code: shinytest2::test_app("path/to/schoolhistory/testapp")

title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: want table teacher can see student-course pair, pick letter grade dropdown, optionally add notes. Three students, two courses, six rows. row_id column uniquely identifies row. student_name class_name display-. student_id class_id travel along silently – ’ll need later saving. Now define table table_config() widget_col(). grade choices plain character vector – simplest format. normalize_choices() converts internally label value string. Wire minimal app. ’s working editable table reset button. Every row gets grade dropdown notes field, rendered inline reactable. module handles input wiring, state tracking, data collection. data disappears app restarts. Let’s fix .
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  roster <- data.frame(   row_id       = c("s1_c1", "s1_c2", "s2_c1", "s2_c2", "s3_c1", "s3_c2"),   student_name = c("Amara Johnson", "Amara Johnson",                    "Ben Martinez",  "Ben Martinez",                    "Clara Chen",    "Clara Chen"),   class_name   = c("Geometry", "Literature",                    "Geometry", "Literature",                    "Geometry", "Literature"),   student_id   = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L),   class_id     = c(1L, 2L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 2L),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = roster$row_id,   row_labels = paste(roster$student_name, "--", roster$class_name),    columns = list(     widget_col("grade", "dropdown", "Grade",       options = list(         choices = c("A+", "A", "A-",                     "B+", "B", "B-",                     "C+", "C", "C-",                     "D+", "D", "D-",                     "F")       )     ),     widget_col("notes", "text", "Notes",       min_width = 200,       options = list(max_chars = 200, placeholder = "Optional notes...")     )   ),    # Show the student-course labels as a badge column   badge_col   = "label",   badge_label = "Student / Course",    show_reset = TRUE,    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       row_id = row_key,       grade  = if (is.null(row_state$grade) || is.na(row_state$grade)) {         NA_character_       } else {         as.character(row_state$grade)       },       notes = row_state$notes %||% "",       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   },    from_saved_fn = function(db_row, col_specs) {     list(       grade = if (!is.null(db_row$grade)) as.character(db_row$grade) else NA_character_,       notes = if (!is.null(db_row$notes)) as.character(db_row$notes) else ""     )   } ) ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Student Grade Entry"),   config_table_ui("grades", cfg) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   config_table_server("grades", cfg) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: ’ll use single SQLite table store grade entries. One row per student-course pair, auto-incrementing ID. UNIQUE(student_id, class_id) constraint means student can one grade per course. ’ll use upserts saving.
code: library(DBI) library(RSQLite)  create_database <- function(db_path = "grades.sqlite") {   con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), db_path)    dbExecute(con, "     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS student_grades (       id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,       student_id INTEGER NOT NULL,       class_id   INTEGER NOT NULL,       grade      TEXT,       notes      TEXT,       UNIQUE(student_id, class_id)     )   ")    dbDisconnect(con)   message("Database created at: ", db_path) }  # Run once create_database()

title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: app starts, check grades saved previously feed back table via data_r. from_saved_fn defined config handles parsing saved row column state config_table_server expects.
code: load_existing <- function(con, roster) {   saved <- dbReadTable(con, "student_grades")   if (nrow(saved) == 0) return(NULL)    saved |>     dplyr::mutate(row_id = paste0("s", student_id, "_c", class_id)) |>     dplyr::select(row_id, grade, notes) }

title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: teacher clicks Save, read module’s get_data() reactive – data frame row_id, grade, notes – upsert row database.
code: save_grades <- function(con, current_data, roster) {   if (is.null(current_data) || nrow(current_data) == 0) return()    to_save <- current_data |>     dplyr::left_join(       roster |> dplyr::select(row_id, student_id, class_id),       by = "row_id"     ) |>     dplyr::filter(!is.na(grade) & nchar(grade) > 0)    if (nrow(to_save) == 0) return()    purrr::walk(seq_len(nrow(to_save)), function(i) {     row <- to_save[i, ]     dbExecute(con, "       INSERT INTO student_grades (student_id, class_id, grade, notes)       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)       ON CONFLICT(student_id, class_id)       DO UPDATE SET grade = ?, notes = ?     ", params = list(       row$student_id, row$class_id, row$grade, row$notes,       row$grade, row$notes     ))   })    showNotification(     paste("Saved", nrow(to_save), "grade entries."),     type = "message"   ) }

title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: Everything wired together. Run create_database() , launch app. Pick grades, add notes, click Save. Restart app – entries still .
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus) library(DBI) library(RSQLite) library(dplyr)  # -- Roster ------------------------------------------------------------------- roster <- data.frame(   row_id       = c("s1_c1", "s1_c2", "s2_c1", "s2_c2", "s3_c1", "s3_c2"),   student_name = c("Amara Johnson", "Amara Johnson",                    "Ben Martinez",  "Ben Martinez",                    "Clara Chen",    "Clara Chen"),   class_name   = c("Geometry", "Literature",                    "Geometry", "Literature",                    "Geometry", "Literature"),   student_id   = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 2L, 3L, 3L),   class_id     = c(1L, 2L, 1L, 2L, 1L, 2L),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # -- Config ------------------------------------------------------------------- cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = roster$row_id,   row_labels = paste(roster$student_name, "--", roster$class_name),    columns = list(     widget_col("grade", "dropdown", "Grade",       options = list(         choices = c("A+", "A", "A-", "B+", "B", "B-",                     "C+", "C", "C-", "D+", "D", "D-", "F")       )     ),     widget_col("notes", "text", "Notes",       min_width = 200,       options = list(max_chars = 200, placeholder = "Optional notes...")     )   ),    badge_col   = "label",   badge_label = "Student / Course",   show_reset  = TRUE,    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       row_id = row_key,       grade  = if (is.null(row_state$grade) || is.na(row_state$grade)) {         NA_character_       } else {         as.character(row_state$grade)       },       notes = row_state$notes %||% "",       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   },    from_saved_fn = function(db_row, col_specs) {     list(       grade = if (!is.null(db_row$grade)) as.character(db_row$grade) else NA_character_,       notes = if (!is.null(db_row$notes)) as.character(db_row$notes) else ""     )   } )  # -- Helpers ------------------------------------------------------------------ load_existing <- function(con, roster) {   saved <- dbReadTable(con, "student_grades")   if (nrow(saved) == 0) return(NULL)    saved |>     mutate(row_id = paste0("s", student_id, "_c", class_id)) |>     select(row_id, grade, notes) }  save_grades <- function(con, current_data, roster) {   if (is.null(current_data) || nrow(current_data) == 0) return()    to_save <- current_data |>     left_join(       roster |> select(row_id, student_id, class_id),       by = "row_id"     ) |>     filter(!is.na(grade) & nchar(grade) > 0)    if (nrow(to_save) == 0) return()    purrr::walk(seq_len(nrow(to_save)), function(i) {     row <- to_save[i, ]     dbExecute(con, "       INSERT INTO student_grades (student_id, class_id, grade, notes)       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)       ON CONFLICT(student_id, class_id)       DO UPDATE SET grade = ?, notes = ?     ", params = list(       row$student_id, row$class_id, row$grade, row$notes,       row$grade, row$notes     ))   })    showNotification(     paste("Saved", nrow(to_save), "grade entries."),     type = "message"   ) }  # -- App ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ui <- fluidPage(   titlePanel("Student Grade Entry"),   config_table_ui("grades", cfg),   br(),   actionButton("save", "Save Grades", class = "btn-primary") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), "grades.sqlite")   onStop(function() dbDisconnect(con))    existing_data <- load_existing(con, roster)    result <- config_table_server("grades", cfg,     data_r = reactive(existing_data)   )    observeEvent(input$save, {     save_grades(con, result$get_data(), roster)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: What the module handles for you
title: Using Config Table with reactablePlus
text: six-row table 12 inputs (6 dropdowns + 6 text fields). table 30 students 5 courses 300. Without config_table_server(), ’d build track one manually, synchronize reactable display, collect values back data frame . module reduces table_config(), couple widget_col() calls, reactive. pattern shown – load database, edit table, save back – generalizes domain: inventory management, survey responses, data annotation, scheduling, anywhere need structured inline editing Shiny.

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("matthewcurrier/reactablePlus")

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: working editable table 30 lines:
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = c("row1", "row2", "row3"),   row_labels = c("Alice", "Bob", "Carol"),    columns = list(     widget_col("role", "dropdown", "Role",       options = list(choices = c("Analyst", "Engineer", "Manager"))     ),     widget_col("score", "numeric", "Score",       options = list(min = 0, max = 100, step = 1)     ),     widget_col("active", "checkbox", "Active"),     widget_col("notes", "text", "Notes",       options = list(placeholder = "Optional notes...", max_chars = 200)     )   ),    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       id     = row_key,       role   = row_state$role %||% NA_character_,       score  = row_state$score %||% NA_real_,       active = isTRUE(row_state$active),       notes  = row_state$notes %||% "",       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   } )  ui <- fluidPage(   config_table_ui("demo", cfg) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   result <- config_table_server("demo", cfg)    observe({     print(result$get_data())   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: reactablePlus organized two layers. Use either independently combine . Layer 1 – Picker widgets. Standalone Shiny inputs (searchPickerInput, attendancePickerInput, homeschoolPickerInput, notesInput, gearPopoverInput) custom JS bindings. Use anywhere ’d use regular Shiny input. Layer 2 – Config-driven table module. Shiny module (config_table_ui / config_table_server) builds editable reactable table_config() object. Supports primitive inputs (dropdown, numeric, date, checkbox, toggle, text) popover picker widgets – gating, row selection, reset, mutual exclusion, fill-. Tables can static (rows fixed config time) dynamic (rows derived reactive source_data data frame, state preservation across changes).

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: widget_col() supports six inline HTML input types. Type-specific options go options list.
code: columns <- list(   # Dropdown -- choices can be a character vector, named vector, or   # list of list(label, value). All three are normalized internally.   widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status",     options = list(       choices = c("Active" = "active", "On Leave" = "leave", "Terminated" = "term")     )   ),    # Numeric -- min is required; max and step are optional   widget_col("hours", "numeric", "Hours",     options = list(min = 0, max = 80, step = 0.5)   ),    # Date -- with optional bounds   widget_col("start", "date", "Start Date",     options = list(min_date = "2020-01-01", max_date = "2030-12-31")   ),    # Checkbox -- no extra options   widget_col("enrolled", "checkbox", "Enrolled"),    # Toggle -- rendered as an on/off button   widget_col("billable", "toggle", "Billable"),    # Text -- with optional max_chars and placeholder   widget_col("notes", "text", "Notes",     options = list(max_chars = 500, placeholder = "Enter notes...")   ) )

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: three normalized via normalize_choices(). Use simplest format fits.
code: # 1. Plain vector -- label equals value widget_col("color", "dropdown", "Color",   options = list(choices = c("Red", "Blue", "Green")) )  # 2. Named vector -- names become display labels widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status",   options = list(choices = c("Active" = "active", "On Leave" = "leave")) )  # 3. List of lists -- for non-character values widget_col("rating", "dropdown", "Rating",   options = list(choices = list(     list(label = "Good", value = 3),     list(label = "OK", value = 2),     list(label = "Poor", value = 1)   )) )

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Use gate parameter widget_col() lock column conditions met. Gates purely server-driven – client-side JavaScript needed. table_config() automatically marks controller columns (, "status") triggers_rerender = TRUE table re-renders change. Gated values enforced server-side get_data() – locked column’s value forced empty_value. Gates can also require row selection: conditions use logic – every condition must pass input unlock.
code: cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = c("r1", "r2", "r3"),   row_labels = c("Item A", "Item B", "Item C"),    columns = list(     widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status",       options = list(choices = c("Active" = "active", "Inactive" = "inactive"))     ),     # Score is locked until Status = "active"     widget_col("score", "numeric", "Score",       options = list(min = 0, max = 100),       gate = list(         list(type = "value", col_id = "status", values = c("active"))       )     )   ),    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       id     = row_key,       status = row_state$status %||% NA_character_,       score  = row_state$score %||% NA_real_,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   } ) widget_col("priority", "dropdown", "Priority",   options = list(choices = c("High", "Medium", "Low")),   gate = list(     list(type = "selected"),     list(type = "value", col_id = "status", values = c("active"))   ) )

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Enable row selection selectable = TRUE reset button show_reset = TRUE: Reset clears inputs empty_value deselects rows single reactive flush.
code: cfg <- table_config(   row_keys   = c("r1", "r2"),   row_labels = c("Item A", "Item B"),   selectable = TRUE,   show_reset = TRUE,    columns = list(     widget_col("notes", "text", "Notes")   ),    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       id       = row_key,       selected = isTRUE(row_state$.selected),       notes    = row_state$notes %||% "",       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   } )  # In the server: result <- config_table_server("demo", cfg)  # result$get_data()     -- reactive data frame with gate enforcement # result$selected_ids() -- reactive character vector of selected row keys

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Static mode (examples ) perfect know rows config time. use cases need rows appear, disappear, change reactively — filtered roster, search results, records database query. Dynamic mode handles . Enable setting row_id_col instead row_keys / row_labels: server, pass reactive data frame source_data. time changes, module derives new row set, preserves user-entered values surviving rows, assigns defaults new ones: row leaves (filter changed) later returns, edits restored — module retains departed rows’ state internally. computed labels, pass function instead column name:
code: students <- data.frame(   student_id = paste0("s", 1:6),   name = c("Amara", "Ben", "Clara", "David", "Elena", "Femi"),   email = paste0(     c("amara", "ben", "clara", "david", "elena", "femi"),     "@school.edu"   ),   dept = c("Eng", "Eng", "Sci", "Sci", "Arts", "Arts"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  cfg <- table_config(   row_id_col    = "student_id",   row_label_col = "name",    columns = list(     widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status",       options = list(choices = c("Active" = "active", "On Leave" = "leave"))     ),     widget_col("score", "numeric", "Score",       options = list(min = 0, max = 100)     )   ),    to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) {     data.frame(       student_id = row_key,       status     = row_state$status %||% NA_character_,       score      = row_state$score %||% NA_real_,       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     )   } ) server <- function(input, output, session) {   filtered <- reactive({     students[students$dept == input$dept_filter, ]   })    result <- config_table_server("demo", cfg,     source_data = filtered   ) } cfg <- table_config(   row_id_col   = "id",   row_label_fn = function(row) paste(row$first, row$last),   columns = list(widget_col("x", "text", "X")) )

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: table needs switch different data contexts (e.g. opening Report B Report ), use reset_signal replace column values selection state atomically: signal ignored NULL (including initial reactiveVal(NULL) startup). non-NULL values trigger state replacement. works static dynamic mode.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   reset <- reactiveVal(NULL)    observeEvent(input$switch_report, {     saved <- load_report(input$report_id)     reset(list(       data     = saved,              # data.frame parsed via from_saved_fn       selected = saved$id[saved$sel] # character vector, or NULL to clear     ))   })    result <- config_table_server("tbl", cfg,     source_data  = filtered,     reset_signal = reset   ) }

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: dynamic mode, often want show read-context source_data alongside editable columns — email address, department, enrollment date. Use display_col(): Display columns appear badge column editable widget columns. accept width, min_width, optional render_fn custom HTML rendering:
code: cfg <- table_config( row_id_col = "student_id", row_label_col = "name", display_cols = list( display_col("email", "Email", min_width = 180), display_col("dept", "Dept", width = 100) ), columns = list( widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status", options = list(choices = c("Active" = "active", "Inactive" = "inactive")) ) ), to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) { data.frame( student_id = row_key, status = row_state$status %||% NA_character_, stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) } ) display_col("status", "Status", render_fn = function(value, row_key) { cls <- if (identical(value, "active")) "text-success" else "text-muted" sprintf('%s', cls, value) })

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: complex inputs – typeahead search, multi-field popovers, rating scales – use picker widget types: Picker widgets require useReactablePlus() UI (config_table_ui() includes automatically) bindPickersOnRender() binding initialization (config_table_server() handles internally).
code: cfg <- table_config( row_keys = c("PK", "K", "01"), row_labels = c("PreK", "K", "1st"), columns = list( widget_col("school", "search_picker", "School", min_width = 300), widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", width = 200), widget_col("notes", "notes_input", "Notes", min_width = 150) ), # Show a grade badge column badge_col = "grade", badge_label = "Grade", badge_render_fn = function(row_key, row_label) { css_class <- paste0("grade-badge g-", row_key) sprintf('%s', css_class, row_label) } )

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Every picker widget works standalone Shiny input outside tables. Call useReactablePlus() UI load JS CSS.
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   searchPickerInput("my_school", grade_key = "PK",                     grade_label = "PreK"),   attendancePickerInput("my_att", grade_label = "PreK"),   notesInput("my_notes", placeholder = "Type here...") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$my_school, {     message("School selected: ", input$my_school$name)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: Embedding widgets inside a custom reactable
title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: placing widgets inside reactable::colDef(cell = ...) outside config_table, two extra steps needed reactable’s React-based rendering doesn’t process Shiny input bindings automatically. Call useReactablePlus() UI load JS/CSS page level. Wrap reactable widget bindPickersOnRender() initialize input bindings table DOM created.
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   reactable::reactableOutput("tbl") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$tbl <- reactable::renderReactable({     tbl <- reactable::reactable(       data.frame(grade = c("PK", "K"), notes = c("", "")),       columns = list(         notes = reactable::colDef(           html = TRUE,           cell = function(value, index) {             as.character(notesInput(               paste0("note_", index),               value = value,               placeholder = "Add a note"             ))           }         )       )     )     bindPickersOnRender(tbl)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: table_config() provides hooks keep core module generic letting domain-specific apps customize appearance: badge_col / badge_label / badge_render_fn – opt-label column custom rendering (e.g., grade badges CSS classes) row_class_fn(row_key, row_state) – custom CSS classes per row to_output_fn(row_state, row_key) – marshal row state data frame output schema from_saved_fn(db_row, col_specs) – parse saved data row state toolbar_stats_fn(rows, row_keys) – dynamic toolbar content year_col / year_range – opt-editable year spinner column row_id_col / row_label_col / row_label_fn – dynamic row mode display_cols – read-columns source_data (dynamic mode)

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: PreK–12 education apps, reactablePlus exports helper functions grade-utils.R: gradeChoices() – named character vector 14-grade ladder gradeLabelMap() – grade key display label lookup gradeIndex() – grade key numeric index ordering gradeInRange() – check grade falls within school’s range convenience exports, required table infrastructure.

title: Getting Started with reactablePlus
text: Browse Reference full documentation every exported function. See table_config() mutual exclusion, fill-, gear toggle configuration. See widget_col() full list types, options, gate conditions.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: reactablePlus extends reactable inline editing widgets declarative configuration layer building editable tables Shiny applications.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: SchoolPicker — typeahead school search popover AttendancePicker — multi-section attendance rating popover HomeschoolPicker — homeschool details popover NotesInput — inline text input Shiny binding GearPopover — settings gear icon toggle switches

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: Shiny module (editable_table_ui / editable_table_server) driven declarative row_spec, col_spec, table_config objects. module handles rendering, input collection, validation, selection gating, reset, data marshaling without domain-specific knowledge.

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("OWNER/reactablePlus")

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
code: library(shiny) library(reactablePlus)  ui <- fluidPage(   useReactablePlus(),   reactableOutput("table") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$table <- renderReactable({     tbl <- reactable(my_data, columns = list(       notes = colDef(html = TRUE, cell = function(value, index) {         as.character(notesInput(paste0("notes_", index), value = value))       })     ))     bindPickersOnRender(tbl)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny
text: MIT

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: Creates attendance picker widget renders dashed-border trigger button empty pill summary filled. Clicking opens popover configurable radio groups optional notes textarea.

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
code: attendancePickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   sections = NULL,   trigger_label = "+ Mark attendance",   popover_title = "Attendance",   show_notes = TRUE,   notes_placeholder = "Absences, tardies, context for this school year…",   width = "100%" )

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value returned server named list keyed section's key, plus notes notes enabled, NULL nothing set. value Optional initial value — named list keyed section keys character values. grade_label Optional display label row's grade (e.g. "3rd grade"). Used trigger's aria-label screen reader context. sections list section definitions popover body. section list : key Character. key returned value list (e.g. "school", "class_"). label Character. heading radio group. levels Character vector. radio options. pill_prefix Character (optional). Text prepended pill display section filled (e.g. "Class: " produces "Class: Excellent"). Default prefix — raw level shown. pill_icon Character (optional). Pill icon style: "school" (default first section), "pencil", NULL icon. impact_display List (optional). present, renders sub-line instead pill section. named entries mapping level values display strings, e.g. list(Yes = "\u26A0 impacts academics", = "academic impact"). Defaults school history attendance sections (School Attendance, Class Attendance, Impacts Academics). trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Mark attendance". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Attendance". show_notes Logical. Whether include notes textarea popover. Default TRUE. notes_placeholder Character. Placeholder notes textarea. Default "Absences, tardies, context school year\u2026". width CSS width. Default "100%".

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: htmltools::tagList suitable use Shiny UI, including inside reactable::colDef(cell = ...) renderers.

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
text: popover's radio sections fully configurable via sections parameter, making widget reusable multi-field rating scenario (behavior tracking, therapy progress, etc.).

title: Attendance picker input — attendancePickerInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default school-history attendance (backward compatible) attendancePickerInput("att_pk", grade_label = "PreK")  # Pre-filled attendancePickerInput("att_k",   value = list(school = "Good", class_ = "Excellent", impacts = "No"),   grade_label = "Kindergarten" )  # Custom sections for behavior tracking attendancePickerInput("beh_pk",   sections = list(     list(key = "behavior", label = "Classroom Behavior",          levels = c("Exemplary", "Meets Expectations",                     "Needs Improvement", "Unsatisfactory")),     list(key = "participation", label = "Participation",          levels = c("Active", "Moderate", "Minimal"))   ),   trigger_label = "+ Rate behavior",   popover_title = "Behavior",   notes_placeholder = "Context, observations\u2026" ) } # }

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: Server function config-driven editable table module. Pairs config_table_ui(). Uses table_config() objects drive picker widget rendering, state management, cross-column interactions.

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
code: config_table_server(   id,   config,   source_data = NULL,   data_r = shiny::reactive(NULL),   reset_signal = NULL,   search_fn = NULL )

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: id Module namespace ID. config table_config object. source_data Reactive returning data frame, NULL. Required dynamic mode (row_id_col set config). source_data() changes, module derives current row set, preserves user-entered values surviving rows, assigns defaults new rows. Ignored static mode. Default NULL. data_r Reactive returning NULL (new/add mode) data frame (edit mode saved rows). Seeds initial values via config's from_saved_fn. dynamic mode, applied top source_data-derived row set. reset_signal Reactive returning NULL named list, NULL disable. reactive fires non-NULL list, module replaces internal state forces full re-render. Supported list fields: data data frame parsed via from_saved_fn, NULL reset values empty_value defaults. selected character vector row keys mark selected, NULL deselect . Ignored selectable FALSE. correct mechanism mid-session context switches (e.g. opening Report B Report ) column values selection state need replaced atomically. recommended calling pattern uses reactiveVal(NULL) set genuine switch occurs: search_fn function (query, limit) → data.frame server-side typeahead search. Required column uses search_picker type. Default NULL.
code: reset <- reactiveVal(NULL)     observeEvent(input$switch_report, {       reset(list(data = load_report(input$report_id),                  selected = NULL))     })     config_table_server("tbl", cfg, reset_signal = reset)

title: Config-driven Editable Table Server — config_table_server
text: list(get_data = reactive, selected_ids = reactive).

what: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
title: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
text: Defines non-editable column whose values come source_data dynamic mode. Display columns provide context alongside editable widget_col() columns — e.g. email address, department name, enrollment date user can see edit.

title: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
code: display_col(id, label, width = NULL, min_width = NULL, render_fn = NULL)

title: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
text: id character(1). name column source_data display. Also used column key reactable data frame. label character(1). column header text. width Integer NULL. Fixed column width pixels. min_width Integer NULL. Minimum column width. render_fn Function NULL. optional custom cell renderer (value, row_key) -> HTML string. NULL (default), values shown plain escaped text.

title: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
text: display_col list (S3 class "display_col").

title: Build a read-only display column specification — display_col
code: display_col("email", "Email", width = 200) #> $id #> [1] "email" #> #> $label #> [1] "Email" #> #> $width #> [1] 200 #> #> $min_width #> NULL #> #> $render_fn #> NULL #> #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "display_col" display_col("department", "Dept", min_width = 120) #> $id #> [1] "department" #> #> $label #> [1] "Dept" #> #> $width #> NULL #> #> $min_width #> [1] 120 #> #> $render_fn #> NULL #> #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "display_col" display_col("status", "Status", render_fn = function(value, row_key) { cls <- if (identical(value, "active")) "badge-success" else "badge-muted" sprintf('%s', cls, value) }) #> $id #> [1] "status" #> #> $label #> [1] "Status" #> #> $width #> NULL #> #> $min_width #> NULL #> #> $render_fn #> function (value, row_key) #> { #> cls <- if (identical(value, "active")) #> "badge-success" #> else "badge-muted" #> sprintf("%s", cls, value) #> } #> #> #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "display_col"

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
code: homeschoolPickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   grade_key = NULL,   providers = c("Mother", "Father", "Grandmother", "Grandfather", "Guardian", "Tutor",     "Co-op", "Online program", "Other"),   provider_label = "Who provided homeschooling?",   curriculum_label = "Curriculum / program",   curriculum_placeholder = "e.g. Time4Learning, Sonlight, custom",   show_curriculum = TRUE,   show_notes = TRUE,   notes_placeholder = "Evaluator, co-op participation, any context…",   trigger_label = "+ Mark homeschool",   trigger_sub_label = "(details optional)",   popover_title = "Homeschool details",   popover_title_sub = "(optional)",   filled_pill_label = "Homeschool",   clear_label = "remove homeschool",   ns = "",   width = "100%" )

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value named list , by_other, curriculum, notes active, NULL inactive. value Optional initial value — list combination fields . Pass list() active details. Pass NULL (default) inactive. grade_label Optional display label accessible naming. grade_key grade's two-char key (e.g. "PK", "03"). providers Character vector provider dropdown options. last element "", additional free-text input shown "" selected. Default: c("Mother", "Father", "Grandmother", "Grandfather", "Guardian", "Tutor", "Co-op", "Online program", ""). provider_label Character. Label provider dropdown. Default "provided homeschooling?". curriculum_label Character. Label curriculum text input. Default "Curriculum / program". curriculum_placeholder Character. Placeholder curriculum field. Default "e.g. Time4Learning, Sonlight, custom". show_curriculum Logical. Whether include curriculum field. Default TRUE. show_notes Logical. Whether include notes textarea. Default TRUE. notes_placeholder Character. Placeholder notes textarea. Default "Evaluator, co-op participation, context\u2026". trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Mark homeschool". trigger_sub_label Character NULL. Sub-label trigger button. Default "(details optional)". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Homeschool details". popover_title_sub Character NULL. Popover header sub-text. Default "(optional)". filled_pill_label Character. Text primary pill active. Default "Homeschool". clear_label Character. Text footer clear/remove link. Default "remove homeschool". ns Shiny module namespace prefix. Default "". width CSS width. Default "100%".

title: Homeschool picker input — homeschoolPickerInput

what: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
text: Converts shorthand choice formats canonical list--lists structure expected widget_col() cell renderers. Called automatically widget_col() validation type = "dropdown", can also used directly.

title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
code: normalize_choices(choices)

title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
text: choices character vector, named character vector, list lists. Empty strings NA values character vectors rejected.

title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
text: list lists, label (character) value.

title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
text: Three formats accepted: Unnamed character vector c("High", "Medium", "Low") — label value identical. Named character vector c("High" = "high", "Medium" = "med") — names become labels, values become values. List lists list(list(label = "High", value = "high"), ...) — canonical format, returned -.

title: Normalize dropdown choices to list-of-lists format — normalize_choices
code: # Unnamed vector — label == value normalize_choices(c("High", "Medium", "Low")) #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$label #> [1] "High" #>  #> [[1]]$value #> [1] "High" #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$label #> [1] "Medium" #>  #> [[2]]$value #> [1] "Medium" #>  #>  #> [[3]] #> [[3]]$label #> [1] "Low" #>  #> [[3]]$value #> [1] "Low" #>  #>   # Named vector — names are labels normalize_choices(c("High" = "high", "Medium" = "med", "Low" = "low")) #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$label #> [1] "High" #>  #> [[1]]$value #> [1] "high" #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$label #> [1] "Medium" #>  #> [[2]]$value #> [1] "med" #>  #>  #> [[3]] #> [[3]]$label #> [1] "Low" #>  #> [[3]]$value #> [1] "low" #>  #>   # Already canonical — returned as-is normalize_choices(list(   list(label = "Good", value = 3),   list(label = "Bad",  value = 1) )) #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$label #> [1] "Good" #>  #> [[1]]$value #> [1] 3 #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$label #> [1] "Bad" #>  #> [[2]]$value #> [1] 1 #>  #>

what: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
title: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
text: Extends 'reactable' inline editing widgets (pickers, popovers, text inputs) declarative configuration layer building editable tables Shiny applications. Provides generic editable table module driven row column specifications, plus library cell-level input widgets custom Shiny bindings.

title: reactablePlus: Enhanced Interactive Tables with Inline Editors for Shiny — reactablePlus-package
text: Maintainer: First Last first.last@example.com

what: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
title: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
text: Launches one bundled demo apps showcase package features. Call arguments see available examples.

title: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
code: runExample(example = NULL, port = NULL)

title: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
text: example Character. name example run. Available examples: "inventory" Product inventory tracker — 6 primitive input types, value-based gating, reset, badge column. "roster" Team roster — row selection, chained selection + value gating, reset, selected_ids output. "evaluations" Student evaluations — attendance_picker custom sections, notes_input, badge_render_fn, row_class_fn, mixing picker primitive columns. port Integer. Port run app . Default NULL (Shiny picks available port).

title: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
text: Called side effect (launches Shiny app).

title: Run a reactablePlus example app — runExample
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # List available examples runExample()  # Launch a specific example runExample("inventory") runExample("roster") runExample("evaluations") } # }

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
text: Creates typeahead search picker widget. empty, renders dashed-border trigger button. filled, renders card showing selected result's details action links (change, fill , clear). schoolPickerInput() deprecated alias searchPickerInput(). removed future release.

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
code: searchPickerInput(   inputId,   value = NULL,   grade_label = NULL,   grade_key = NULL,   show_nces_id = TRUE,   trigger_label = "+ Pick school",   popover_title = "Find school",   search_placeholder = "Search by school name, city, district…",   empty_hint = "Type 2+ characters to search 100k+ US schools",   no_match_hint = "No schools match. Check spelling — picking is required.",   show_fill_down = TRUE,   ns = "",   width = "100%" )  schoolPickerInput(...)

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. value named list id, name, district, city, state, type, NULL. value Optional initial value — list school fields . grade_label Optional display label (e.g. "3rd grade") accessible naming. grade_key grade's two-char key (e.g. "PK", "03"). Used fill-action tell server grade triggered . show_nces_id Logical. Whether show NCES ID filled state search results. Default TRUE. trigger_label Character. Text empty-state trigger button. Default "+ Pick school". popover_title Character. Popover header text. Default "Find school". search_placeholder Character. Placeholder search input. Default "Search school name, city, district\u2026". empty_hint Character. Hint text shown user types. Default "Type 2+ characters search 100k+ US schools". no_match_hint Character. Text shown search returns results. Default "schools match. Check spelling \u2014 picking required.". show_fill_down Logical. Whether show "fill " action link filled state. Default TRUE. ns Character. Shiny module namespace prefix search communication. Pass session$ns("") within module. top-level apps, leave "". width CSS width. Default "100%". ... Arguments passed searchPickerInput().

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
text: htmltools::tagList.

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
text: Search server-side: widget sends queries R via input$<ns>school_search; use useTypeaheadSearch() server function wire response. Trigger popover labels configurable widget can used server-side typeahead scenario (school search, provider lookup, facility search, etc.).

title: Search picker input — searchPickerInput
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # School search (default labels) searchPickerInput("school_PK", grade_label = "PreK", grade_key = "PK")  # Custom labels for provider search searchPickerInput("provider_PK",   trigger_label = "+ Find provider",   popover_title = "Search providers",   search_placeholder = "Type provider name or specialty\u2026",   empty_hint = "Type 2+ characters to search providers",   no_match_hint = "No providers found.",   show_nces_id = FALSE,   show_fill_down = FALSE,   grade_label = "PreK",   grade_key = "PK" ) } # }

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: Creates declarative configuration object drives config_table_ui() / config_table_server(). config describes rows columns appear, picker widget column uses, cross-column interactions (mutual exclusion, fill-, gating) work.

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
code: table_config(   row_keys = NULL,   row_labels = NULL,   columns,   row_id_col = NULL,   row_label_col = NULL,   row_label_fn = NULL,   display_cols = NULL,   selectable = FALSE,   show_reset = FALSE,   gear_toggles = NULL,   interactions = list(),   from_saved_fn = NULL,   to_output_fn = NULL,   toolbar_stats_fn = NULL,   search_fn_col = NULL,   badge_col = NULL,   badge_label = "Label",   badge_render_fn = NULL,   row_class_fn = NULL,   year_col = NULL,   year_range = c(1990L, 2050L) )

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: row_keys Character vector row identifiers (e.g. grade keys). Required static mode. dynamic mode, defaults character(0) — rows derived source_data runtime. row_labels Character vector display labels (length row_keys). Required static mode. dynamic mode, defaults character(0) — labels derived via row_label_col row_label_fn. columns List column specs built widget_col(). row_id_col character(1) NULL. name column source_data uniquely identifies row. non-NULL config operates dynamic mode. Default NULL (static mode). row_label_col character(1) NULL. name column source_data use display label. Ignored row_id_col NULL. Exactly one row_label_col row_label_fn must supplied dynamic mode. row_label_fn function NULL. function (source_data_row) -> character(1) computes display label single row (one-row data frame) source_data. Ignored row_id_col NULL. Exactly one row_label_col row_label_fn must supplied dynamic mode. display_cols List display_col() specs, NULL. Read-columns whose values drawn source_data dynamic mode. Displayed badge column editable widget columns. Requires dynamic mode (row_id_col must set). Default NULL (display columns). selectable Logical. TRUE, checkbox column prepended row selection tracked. Required column gate condition type = "selected". Default FALSE. show_reset Logical. TRUE, Reset button shown toolbar clears inputs deselects rows. Default FALSE. gear_toggles Named list toggle definitions gearPopoverInput(). Pass NULL gear icon. interactions List optional mutual_exclusion fill_down entries. See Details. from_saved_fn Function (db_row, col_specs) -> named list. Converts one row saved data row-state list keyed column ID. Called per row data_r() provides data frame. to_output_fn Function (row_state, row_key) -> single-row data.frame. Converts one row state back output schema. toolbar_stats_fn Function (rows, row_keys) -> shiny::HTML string toolbar. Pass NULL toolbar. search_fn_col Character. Column ID requires server-side search (.e. uses useTypeaheadSearch()). Pass NULL column needs search. Default NULL. badge_col Character NULL. non-NULL, static label column shown using row_labels. Default NULL (badge). badge_label Character. Column header badge column. Default "Label". Ignored badge_col NULL. badge_render_fn Function (row_key, row_label) -> HTML string badge cell. Default NULL renders row_label. Supply custom function domain-specific styling (e.g. grade badges CSS classes). row_class_fn Function (row_key, row_state) -> CSS class string NULL. Applied per row via reactable's rowClass. Default NULL (row classes). year_col Character NULL. non-NULL, editable year spinner column shown. Default NULL (year column). year_range Integer vector length 2, e.g. c(1990, 2050).

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: table_config list (S3 class "table_config").

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: Two modes supported: Static mode (default): rows fixed config time via row_keys / row_labels. Use set editable rows known front (e.g. grade roster). Dynamic mode: rows derived runtime reactive source_data data frame passed config_table_server(). Enable supplying row_id_col either row_label_col row_label_fn. mode row_keys / row_labels optional (default character(0)).

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: interactions$mutual_exclusion: list rules, list when_on (column ID), clears (column ID), display (HTML string show cleared column's cell). interactions$fill_down: list column (column ID), range_check_fn (optional function (row_key, value) -> logical returns TRUE row range fill-), optionally input_name (character — Shiny input name widget JS sends; defaults paste0(column, "_fill_down")).

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: Columns gate conditions (set via widget_col(gate = ...)) automatically disabled conditions met. Controller columns referenced gate conditions automatically marked triggers_rerender = TRUE table updates change.

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
text: row_id_col non-NULL, config dynamic mode. Rows fixed config time — derived source_data reactive runtime. time source_data changes, module merges new row set existing state: user-entered values rows survive preserved, new rows receive empty_value defaults, departed rows' state retained internally restores rows reappear.

title: Build a table configuration — table_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Static mode — rows fixed at config time cfg <- table_config( row_keys = c("PK", "K", "01"), row_labels = c("PreK", "K", "1st"), columns = list( widget_col("school", "search_picker", "School", min_width = 300), widget_col("attendance", "attendance_picker", "Attendance", width = 200) ), badge_col = "grade", badge_label = "Grade", badge_render_fn = function(row_key, row_label) { css_class <- paste0("grade-badge g-", row_key) sprintf('%s', css_class, row_label) } ) # Dynamic mode — rows derived from source_data at runtime cfg_dyn <- table_config( row_id_col = "student_id", row_label_col = "student_name", columns = list( widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status", options = list(choices = c("Active", "Inactive")) ), widget_col("score", "numeric", "Score", options = list(min = 0, max = 100) ) ), selectable = TRUE, to_output_fn = function(row_state, row_key) { data.frame( id = row_key, status = row_state$status %||% NA_character_, score = row_state$score %||% NA_real_, stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) } ) } # }

what: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: Defines single column table_config(). column maps either picker widget primitive inline input carries type-specific options.

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
code: widget_col(   id,   type,   label,   width = NULL,   min_width = NULL,   options = list(),   gate = NULL,   gear_toggle = NULL,   triggers_rerender = FALSE,   empty_value = NULL,   render_cell_fn = NULL,   validate_fn = NULL )

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: id Character. Column ID — becomes key row state prefix Shiny input IDs (e.g. "school" -> input$school_PK). type Character. One : Picker widgets (complex popover-based inputs): "search_picker", "attendance_picker", "homeschool_picker", "notes_input". Primitive inputs (inline HTML form controls): "dropdown", "numeric", "date", "checkbox", "toggle", "text". Extensible: "custom" (requires render_cell_fn). label Character. Column header text. width Integer NULL. Fixed column width pixels. min_width Integer NULL. Minimum column width. options Named list type-specific options: Picker widgets: passed widget constructor (e.g. list(show_nces_id = TRUE) search picker, list(sections = ...) attendance picker). dropdown: choices (required — character vector, named character vector, list list(label, value); see normalize_choices()), placeholder (default "-- Select --"). numeric: min (required), max, step. date: min_date, max_date (YYYY-MM-DD strings). text: max_chars, placeholder. checkbox / toggle: extra options. gate list conditions must pass column's input enabled. condition fails, input renders locked (disabled + dimmed) value forced empty_value get_data(). Two condition types supported: list(type = "value", col_id = "status", values = c("active")) — column whose id matches col_id must value values row. list(type = "selected") — row must selected (requires selectable = TRUE table_config()). Default NULL (gating — always enabled). gear_toggle Character NULL. gear toggle key controls column's visibility. toggle , column hidden (set show = FALSE). triggers_rerender Logical. TRUE, changes column bump render key (forcing full table re-render). Needed columns involved mutual exclusion. Automatically set TRUE table_config() columns act gate controllers. Default FALSE. empty_value default value empty row. NULL (default), type-appropriate blank used: NA dropdown/date, options$min (0) numeric, FALSE checkbox/toggle, "" text/notes_input, NULL pickers. render_cell_fn Function (ns, row_key, row_state, col_spec, settings) -> HTML string. needed type = "custom". validate_fn Function (value) -> validated value. Controls raw input cleaned storage. Default NULL uses type-appropriate validation.

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
text: widget_col list (S3 class "widget_col").

title: Build a widget column specification — widget_col
code: # Primitive dropdown widget_col("status", "dropdown", "Status",   options = list(choices = c("Active", "Inactive", "Pending")) ) #> $id #> [1] "status" #>  #> $type #> [1] "dropdown" #>  #> $label #> [1] "Status" #>  #> $width #> NULL #>  #> $min_width #> NULL #>  #> $options #> $options$choices #> $options$choices[[1]] #> $options$choices[[1]]$label #> [1] "Active" #>  #> $options$choices[[1]]$value #> [1] "Active" #>  #>  #> $options$choices[[2]] #> $options$choices[[2]]$label #> [1] "Inactive" #>  #> $options$choices[[2]]$value #> [1] "Inactive" #>  #>  #> $options$choices[[3]] #> $options$choices[[3]]$label #> [1] "Pending" #>  #> $options$choices[[3]]$value #> [1] "Pending" #>  #>  #>  #>  #> $gate #> NULL #>  #> $gear_toggle #> NULL #>  #> $triggers_rerender #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $empty_value #> [1] NA #>  #> $render_cell_fn #> NULL #>  #> $validate_fn #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "widget_col"  # Numeric gated by a dropdown value widget_col("score", "numeric", "Score",   options = list(min = 0, max = 100),   gate = list(     list(type = "value", col_id = "status", values = c("active"))   ) ) #> $id #> [1] "score" #>  #> $type #> [1] "numeric" #>  #> $label #> [1] "Score" #>  #> $width #> NULL #>  #> $min_width #> NULL #>  #> $options #> $options$min #> [1] 0 #>  #> $options$max #> [1] 100 #>  #>  #> $gate #> $gate[[1]] #> $gate[[1]]$type #> [1] "value" #>  #> $gate[[1]]$col_id #> [1] "status" #>  #> $gate[[1]]$values #> [1] "active" #>  #>  #>  #> $gear_toggle #> NULL #>  #> $triggers_rerender #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $empty_value #> [1] 0 #>  #> $render_cell_fn #> NULL #>  #> $validate_fn #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "widget_col"  # Picker widget widget_col("school", "search_picker", "School", min_width = 300) #> $id #> [1] "school" #>  #> $type #> [1] "search_picker" #>  #> $label #> [1] "School" #>  #> $width #> NULL #>  #> $min_width #> [1] 300 #>  #> $options #> list() #>  #> $gate #> NULL #>  #> $gear_toggle #> NULL #>  #> $triggers_rerender #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $empty_value #> NULL #>  #> $render_cell_fn #> NULL #>  #> $validate_fn #> NULL #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "widget_col"
